title: My Volunteer Day
summary: A narrator describes a Saturday she spent volunteering at a local community garden, explaining what she did, the people she met, and how the experience made her feel.
body: Last month, I signed up to volunteer at a community garden near my neighbourhood. I had seen a poster about it at the library and thought it sounded like a great way to spend a Saturday. On the morning of the event, I arrived at eight o'clock and met the coordinator, a kind woman named Rosa. She explained what needed to be done and gave everyone a pair of gloves and a task. My job was to help clear a section of the garden that was overgrown with weeds. It was hard work, but I enjoyed it because I was working alongside other volunteers and we chatted the whole time. After two hours, the section looked completely different. We had removed all the weeds and turned the soil, so it was ready for planting. At lunchtime, Rosa brought sandwiches and juice for everyone, and we sat on the grass and talked about our lives. I met a retired teacher, a young student, and a family with two children. Everyone had come for different reasons, but we all shared the same goal of improving the garden for the community. In the afternoon, we planted vegetables including tomatoes, carrots, and herbs. By the end of the day, I felt proud of what we had achieved together. Volunteering gave me a sense of purpose, and I have already signed up to go again next month.

annotations.exampleEn: She signed up to volunteer at the local food bank.

annotations.exampleEn: Helping others gives her a real sense of purpose.
The community garden was open to all residents of the neighbourhood.

grammarNotes.title: Past perfect (had + past participle) for earlier completed actions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Th√¨ qu√° kh·ª© ho√Ýn th√Ýnh (had + qu√° kh·ª© ph√¢n t·ª´) di·ªÖn t·∫£ h√Ýnh ƒë·ªông ƒë√£ x·∫£y ra v√Ý ho√Ýn th√Ýnh tr∆∞·ªõc m·ªôt th·ªùi ƒëi·ªÉm kh√°c trong qu√° kh·ª©. V√≠ d·ª•: 'We had removed all the weeds and turned the soil, so it was ready for planting.' H√Ýnh ƒë·ªông d·ªçn c·ªè v√Ý cu·ªëc ƒë·∫•t x·∫£y ra tr∆∞·ªõc khi v∆∞·ªùn s·∫µn s√Ýng ƒë·ªÉ tr·ªìng.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The past perfect (had + past participle) describes an action completed before another past event. Example: 'We had removed all the weeds and turned the soil, so it was ready for planting.' The removal happened before the ground became ready.

grammarNotes.title: So ‚Äî result clause connector
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'So' k·∫øt n·ªëi nguy√™n nh√¢n v√Ý k·∫øt qu·∫£ trong c√¢u. Ph·∫ßn tr∆∞·ªõc 'so' l√Ý l√Ω do, ph·∫ßn sau l√Ý k·∫øt qu·∫£. V√≠ d·ª•: 'We had turned the soil, so it was ready for planting' ‚Äî vi·ªác cu·ªëc ƒë·∫•t d·∫´n ƒë·∫øn k·∫øt qu·∫£ l√Ý ƒë·∫•t s·∫µn s√Ýng. 'So' kh√°c v·ªõi 'because': 'because' ƒë·ª©ng tr∆∞·ªõc nguy√™n nh√¢n, c√≤n 'so' ƒë·ª©ng tr∆∞·ªõc k·∫øt qu·∫£.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'So' connects a cause to its result. The clause before 'so' is the reason; the clause after is the consequence. Example: 'we had turned the soil, so it was ready for planting.' Compare with 'because': 'because' introduces the cause ('because we turned the soil'), while 'so' introduces the result.

questions.prompt: Where did the narrator first learn about the volunteer event?
questions.explanation: She says 'I had seen a poster about it at the library.'
questions.hint: She saw a poster somewhere specific.

questions.prompt: What time did the narrator arrive at the garden?
questions.explanation: She says 'I arrived at eight o'clock.'

questions.prompt: What did the coordinator give everyone before they started?
options: A pair of gloves and a task
questions.explanation: Rosa 'gave everyone a pair of gloves and a task.'
questions.hint: She gave both a protective item and an assignment.

questions.prompt: What was the narrator's specific task?
options: Clearing a section overgrown with weeds
questions.explanation: Her job 'was to help clear a section of the garden that was overgrown with weeds.'
questions.hint: She had to deal with unwanted plants.

questions.prompt: Why did the narrator enjoy the hard work?
options: Because she was working alongside volunteers and chatting
questions.explanation: She 'enjoyed it because I was working alongside other volunteers and we chatted the whole time.'
questions.hint: The social aspect made it enjoyable.

questions.prompt: How long did it take to clear the weeds?
questions.explanation: 'After two hours, the section looked completely different.'
questions.hint: A specific amount of time is mentioned.

questions.prompt: What did Rosa provide at lunchtime?
questions.explanation: Rosa 'brought sandwiches and juice for everyone.'

questions.prompt: Who did the narrator meet during lunch? (Choose the option listing all three.)
options: A doctor, a student, and a retired nurse
A retired teacher, a young student, and a family with two children
A gardener, a chef, and a teacher
Two retired teachers and a student
questions.explanation: She met 'a retired teacher, a young student, and a family with two children.'
questions.hint: Three different types of people are named.

questions.prompt: What did all the volunteers have in common?
options: They all lived in the same street
They all shared the same goal of improving the garden
questions.explanation: She says 'we all shared the same goal of improving the garden for the community.'
questions.hint: Despite different backgrounds, they had one thing in common.

questions.prompt: What vegetables did they plant in the afternoon?
questions.explanation: 'we planted vegetables including tomatoes, carrots, and herbs.'

questions.prompt: How did the narrator feel at the end of the day?
options: Proud of what they had achieved together
Disappointed because the work was too hard
questions.explanation: She 'felt proud of what we had achieved together.'
questions.hint: Her emotion is a positive one linked to their collective work.

questions.prompt: What did volunteering give the narrator?
questions.explanation: 'Volunteering gave me a sense of purpose.'
questions.hint: It is an emotional or personal reward.

cloze.template: Last month, I {{b1}} to volunteer at a community garden near my neighbourhood. I had seen a poster about it at the {{b2}} and thought it sounded like a great way to spend a Saturday. On the morning of the event, I arrived at eight o'clock and met the {{b3}}, a kind woman named Rosa. My job was to help clear a section of the garden that was {{b4}} with weeds. It was hard work, but I enjoyed it because I was working {{b5}} other volunteers and we chatted the whole time. After two hours, we had removed all the weeds and {{b6}} the soil, so it was ready for planting. At lunchtime, Rosa brought {{b7}} and juice for everyone. I met a retired teacher, a young student, and a family with two children. Everyone had come for different reasons, but we all shared the same {{b8}} of improving the garden for the community. In the afternoon, we {{b9}} vegetables including tomatoes, carrots, and herbs. By the end of the day, I felt {{b10}} of what we had achieved together. Volunteering gave me a sense of {{b11}}, and I have already signed up to go again next month.

blanks.explanation: 'signed up' ‚Äî to sign up means to register or enrol for something.
'library' ‚Äî she saw the poster at the library.
'coordinator' ‚Äî Rosa was the coordinator who organised the work.
'overgrown' ‚Äî overgrown means covered with uncontrolled plant growth.
'alongside' ‚Äî working alongside means working next to someone as a team.
'turned' ‚Äî to turn the soil means to dig it over to prepare it for planting.
'sandwiches' ‚Äî Rosa brought sandwiches and juice for lunch.
'goal' ‚Äî they shared the same goal of improving the garden for the community.
'planted' ‚Äî in the afternoon they planted vegetables.
'proud' ‚Äî she felt proud of what they had achieved together.
'purpose' ‚Äî a sense of purpose means feeling that your actions are meaningful.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator says that volunteering gave her a sense of purpose. Do you think volunteering is important for individuals and for society? What benefits can volunteering bring to both the volunteer and the community?
